positive: Define a revenue metric on top of fct_orders.
Add a customer_region dimension to the customer entity.
Maintain the dbt semantic models as the warehouse changes.
Build dbt semantic models (MetricFlow) for the orders mart.
Preview this semantic model in Viz.
negative: What's in this warehouse and which tables matter?
Profile the events table and flag PII.
Build a staging model for raw orders.
Refactor this dbt model and add tests.

evals.prompt: Define a revenue metric and a couple of dimensions on top of my orders mart as dbt semantic models.
evals.expected_output: Semantic-layer edits written as dbt semantic models (MetricFlow YAML) in the dbt project, presented as a reviewable diff.
assertions: The dbt project is the source of truth; edits are diffs to dbt semantic YAML, not a parallel model
Emitted dbt semantic models are valid MetricFlow YAML
PII flags propagate into emitted dbt (model and column meta); no example values
Nothing is applied silently; the change is a reviewable diff

evals.prompt: Someone hand-edited the dbt semantic model. Maintain it and add a metric without clobbering their changes.
evals.expected_output: Human edits treated as authoritative; the new metric added as a diff; conflicts surfaced and asked about.
assertions: Human dbt edits are authoritative by construction (dbt is the source of truth)
Conflicts surface as a diff and ask; never a silent overwrite
The added metric is valid MetricFlow YAML in the existing semantic model
